meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage user onboarding to overcome a lack of marketing skills. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: If you lack marketing skills, stop chasing traffic and start perfecting your product's user onboarding. A high-converting onboarding flow acts as your silent salesperson, turning every visitor into a power user. By automating value discovery, you retain users longer and build the social proof necessary to grow organically without paid ads.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a deployed project is daunting, but the silence that follows is worse. As a student developer, you've mastered the code, but you're hitting the 'marketing wall'—a common trap where brilliant features sit unused because users don't know where to start. You don't need a massive ad budget; you need a funnel that educates and converts from the very first click.

This case study flips the script. We are moving away from traditional marketing tactics and focusing on 'Product-Led Growth' through intelligent onboarding. By using Antigravity to structure your data flow and Claude Code to accelerate implementation, you will transform your project from a static portfolio piece into a sticky, high-retention application.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the specific feature that provides immediate value. This is the core of your onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my app logic and identify the single most impactful feature that provides 'Aha!' value. Draft a 3-step welcome modal flow that guides a new user to trigger this feature immediately.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate the React components for a 3-step onboarding walkthrough based on the analyzed workflow.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Checklist
steps.description: Create an interactive progress tracker that motivates users to complete their profile or first task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a persistent user onboarding checklist state. Include logic for 'completed' vs 'pending' states and a progress bar calculation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/components/OnboardingChecklist.tsx --prompt 'Implement the checklist state logic and integrate with local storage.'

steps.title: Automate Value Triggers
steps.description: Use event-driven triggers to guide users only when they stall, preventing user fatigue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an event-listener architecture that detects user inactivity for 15 seconds and triggers a contextual tooltip pointing to the main action button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement the inactivity timer hook and trigger the tooltip component.'

steps.title: Implement Data-Driven Feedback
steps.description: Capture user input during onboarding to personalize the app experience instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a user-preference form that captures three key data points during onboarding and maps them to initial dashboard settings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/hooks/useUserPreferences.ts --prompt 'Build the preference capture form and state update logic.'

steps.title: Final QA and Edge-Case Hardening
steps.description: Ensure the onboarding doesn't break if the user skips steps or refreshes the page.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my onboarding code for race conditions. Suggest error handling for scenarios where the user refreshes mid-onboarding.

pro_tips: Never force a sign-up before showing value; let the user interact with the core feature first.
Use micro-copy that focuses on the user's benefit, not the app's features (e.g., 'Get insights' vs 'Submit form').
Keep your onboarding under 60 seconds; if it takes longer, break it into smaller, asynchronous tasks.
Always provide an 'Exit' or 'Skip' button to prevent user frustration.
Track the drop-off rate at each step of your onboarding using simple console logs to identify where users get confused.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, deploy your app to a portfolio platform like Vercel or Netlify. Then, reach out to 5 users for feedback. Use the data from their sessions to iterate on your onboarding flow, making it even tighter and more intuitive.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn any project into a professional-grade SaaS experience without needing a marketing degree. Focus on the user's journey, and the growth will follow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
